summary: Solid sound‑board capture with a stellar Help > Slip > Frank sequence and a punchy second set highlighted by He's Gone, High Time and Crazy Fingers.
review: The June 22 1993 Deer Creek show is praised for its tight first set and an epic second set where the Help > Slip > Frank segue ignites the crowd. Reviewers love Jerry’s soaring solos, Vince’s tasteful keys, Phil’s confident vocals and especially the drum work, while the soundboard source provides a generally clear mix despite occasional click artifacts noted on “High Time.”

ai_rating.rationale: The majority of reviewers rate the show 4‑5 stars and highlight many musical high points; recording quality is described as good with only minor artifact complaints.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Generally clear and well‑balanced, but a few listeners note click/artifact issues on “High Time” that may stem from down‑sampling.

standout_songs: Help on the Way > Slipknot! > Franklin's Tower
show_quality.setlist_flow: Strong first set builds into an epic second set; the Help‑Slip‑Frank segue creates a high‑energy peak, and the closing jam after Crazy Fingers sustains momentum.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Frequent praise for his solos – fingerpicking on Help/Slip/Frank, soaring lead on High Time, and an intense finish on Days Between (though one reviewer felt the last verse was a bit butchered).
band_member_comments.Phil: Noted singing on All Over Now and praised for confident vocal delivery; generally mentioned as being in top form.
band_member_comments.Bob: Mixed remarks – credited with keeping positive vibes on “All Over Now” but also critiqued for briefly “dropping the ball.”
band_member_comments.Keys: Vince’s keyboard work highlighted repeatedly, especially a barrel‑house piano solo on All Over Now and tasteful fills before verses of Help/Slip/Frank.
band_member_comments.Drums: The drum performance is singled out as a highlight; reviewers encourage listening with headphones to appreciate the power of the second‑set drumming.
